response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned full-stack engineer with genuine staff-level credentials and emerging AI tooling experience (MCP code generators, agentic workflows), but the role demands 8+ years of production AI delivery — a bar he falls well short of given that his AI-adjacent work begins only in April 2025. Python, the lingua franca of the AI stack, is absent from his skills list. There is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI, all of which are either required or strongly preferred for this position.
strengths: Hands-on Model Context Protocol (MCP) server and agentic-workflow infrastructure work at re:cinq directly aligns with a listed key technology
Long track record of staff-level technical ownership across diverse stacks (TypeScript, Node.js, AWS CDK, microservices, data pipelines) demonstrates breadth and seniority
Public workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects show technical leadership and a knowledge-sharing ethos consistent with the mentorship expectations of the role
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume and skills list — a critical gap for a GenAI engineering role where the ecosystem is Python-first
No demonstrable experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation, or named multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.), all of which are core requirements
AI and GenAI work is confined to approximately 13 months at one employer, far below the 8-year minimum for production-grade AI solution delivery
